model: ProofCast editorial (composed from TxLINE-verified events + web2 names)
text:
Quarter-final · 11 July 2026 · Miami. Final score: Norway 1–2 England. Billed as Haaland versus Kane, it was Jude Bellingham who decided it — and ended Norway's fairytale.

Two of the tournament's most feared number nines shared the marquee, but the headlines belonged to someone else entirely.

The goals, in sequence: 36′ Andreas Schjelderup — 1–0; 47′ Jude Bellingham — 1–1; 93′ Jude Bellingham — 1–2. That is 3 goals in all, Norway accounting for 1 and England 2.

Andreas Schjelderup gave Norway a deserved lead late in the first half, and the dream of a first semi-final flickered. Bellingham levelled in first-half stoppage time with a driving run and a cool finish. He struck again in extra time to send England to their first semi-final since 2018 and bring Erling Haaland's remarkable run to a shocking end. Neither Haaland nor Kane found the net; the game's biggest stage found a different hero.

Momentum, as ever in the knockouts, proved a fragile and fleeting thing.

The expected-goals picture tightened around every set piece and transition.

For the record, England advanced. Goal, team and minute for every event above are Merkle-proven on Solana devnet via TxLINE; the scorer names are attributed from public match reports and aligned to the verified goals by team and order. Nothing here rests on the commentator's word alone.
